items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building is cleared to build at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard. Data centers generate significant tax revenue and jobs while requiring substantial power and cooling infrastructure that can strain local utilities.

items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B site work permitted on Rue Ferrari
items.summary: A $35 million site work permit issued for Phase B of the PLD Athena project at 5853 Rue Ferrari. This appears to be a multi-phase development; Phase B's scale suggests a major mixed-use or industrial buildout underway.

items.headline: Agreement signed for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for an artwork titled Seeds of Peace Monument by artist Mario Chiodo, to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art installation at the riverfront park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District FY 2026-27 budget approved
items.summary: City Council took action on the FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which funds improvements and services in that commercial area.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative position responsible for day-to-day city operations and execution of council policies.

items.headline: City updates water shortage contingency and master plans
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission heard an overview of the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plans address how Sunnyvale will manage water resources and respond to shortages, relevant as California faces ongoing drought uncertainty.

items.headline: Spring cycle community grants and events announced
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications for the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can learn which local projects and events received funding through this program.

items.headline: Council to hear residential electrification program principles for air quality
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to review proposed principles for residential electrification programs intended to help Palo Alto comply with regional air quality regulations. The presentation will include a status update on the city's electrification efforts.
